items.title: Range query in a sorted event log
items.scenario: Find every event between timestamps t0 and t1 in a sorted list of 10M log lines.
items.explanation: `bisect.bisect_left(times, t0)` and `bisect.bisect_right(times, t1)` give the slice indices in 2 × log₂(10M) ≈ 46 steps instead of scanning all 10M rows.

items.title: Smallest k satisfying a quality threshold
items.scenario: Find the smallest top-k that still gives ≥ 95% recall on your eval set.
items.explanation: Recall is monotonic in k (larger k cannot hurt recall), so binary-search k ∈ [1, K_max]: 'is recall(k) ≥ 95%?' is the predicate.

items.title: Largest temperature that keeps a guardrail satisfied
items.scenario: Higher temperature → more diversity → eventually fails a quality bar.
items.explanation: Sample at temperature t; pass/fail is monotonic in t (mostly true in practice); binary-search the largest t that still passes.

items.title: IVF bucket lookup
items.scenario: An IVF vector index keeps centroid distances sorted; finding the right bucket is a binary-search step.
items.explanation: log₂(n_clusters) probes to locate the right cluster vs n_clusters scans.
